title: Sub-ppm determination of thallium(I) in biological and complex materials
author: Sukumar Chatterjee
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
School of Studies in Chemistry, Pt. Ravishankar Shukla University, Raipur-492 010, India 

  Manuscript received 22 January 2001, revised 16 June 2001. accepted II August 2001  

 Leucocrystal violet has been used as a chromogenic reagent for the determination of thallium( I) in biological lind industrial effluent. The methodology involves the oxidation of T1 I  to T1 III  with bromine water and the resulting T1 III  liberating iodine from potassium iodide in acidic medium. The liberated iodine reacts with leucocrystal violet to form crystal violet dye, which is measured at 590 nm. In aqueous medium, the system obeys Beer's law in the range 2-20 , u g per 25 ml (0.08-0.8 ppm) while in extraction medium the range is 0.5-5 , µ g in 100 ml (0.005-0.05 ppm). The apparent molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity are found to be 2.21 x 10 5  dm 3  mol- 1  cm- 1  and 0.00092 , µ g cm- 2 , respectively.
